Regarding controllable text generation, we perform
automatic and human evaluations. Automatic m
include perplexity, classifier accuracy, and diversi
versity is measured by the number of unique n-g
and we report Dist-1, Dist-2, and Dist-3, which
spond to the proportion of distinct unigrams, big
and trigrams, respectively.
For human evaluation, we assess fluency and top
evance.  Fluency is rated on a 5-point Likert
where higher scores indicate greater linguistic n
ness. Topic relevance is judged by human anno
who determine whether the generated text align
the intended condition, and we report the human
accuracy as a quantitative measure.
Baselines  For likelihood modeling, we benchma
approach against a diverse set of generative mode
lines spanning multiple paradigms. These includ
toregressive models, such as IAF/SCF [62], AR A
Flow [21], Discrete Flow [52], Transformer AR [54
OmniNetT [51]. We also compare with Any-ord
toregressive models, including ARDM [20] and
[49]. Within the diffusion-based paradigm, we
uate against prominent discrete diffusion models
as Multinomial Diffusion [21], D3PM [3], SEDD
MDLM [40], MD4 [45], and RDLM [22]. We a
clude the semi-autoregressive model BD3-LM [2]

port the Bits Per Character (BPC) results in Ta

iff clearly outperforms existing diffusion-based m
and BD3-LM.
